rationale: Read the real code for all four legs. R1: the bug is exactly as claimed — resolvePanelGateFacts (panel.go:302-306) sets beadID="" for non-bead, ResolveGateFacts (gate.go:372) rev-parses literal "bead/", MissingRef short-circuits leg (5) with empty-interpolated message. Resolve-from-target composes: leg (6) needs f.HeadSHA+p.ReviewedHeadSHA (both available from the recorded target); leg (7) worktree resolver returns "" for empty beadID so dirty is correctly skipped; legs (8)-(10) become reachable. The complete/instruct fence HOLDS: complete matches only via panel.ForBead (bead panels, non-empty beadID) so its bead-path rev-parse is untouched; instruct builds its own zeroed non-bead facts and never re-derives staleness. R3: Panel.Gate is genuinely decision-inert (PanelGateDecision/ApproveThreshold never read it); omitempty preserves the R9 byte-identical-when-absent contract; PanelGateExpectedReviewers/ApproveThresholdExpr are exported+read-only; advisory read-through via PanelGateAdvisoryDefault confirmed at config.go:832. R4: code ALREADY resolves-to-family (model() at 139-144; validateReviewerEntries refuses only neither-set) — doc+test-only, minimal, correct given typed-string can't distinguish absent from empty. R2: adding bare $ to SHELL_METACHAR_RE is strictly monotone; nothing legitimate contains $. All fences sound.
findings: R1 design note (non-blocking, plan must heed): instruct's PanelStateEntry.verdict() (panelstate.go:135) consumes d.Message, and for an INCOMPLETE non-bead panel it already renders leg-(8)'s RawMergeFence("") empty-bead fragment today. R1's message hygiene must therefore be implemented in the CLI rendering layer (renderPanelVerify/renderPanelTally/tallyExitAction synthesizing a target-named message for facts.BeadID=="", as instruct already does for the Allow reason) and MUST NOT alter PanelGateDecision's shared Message for the facts instruct produces — otherwise the AC-global 'instruct tests pass unmodified' fence and the R1 falsification ('instruct non-bead handling changes') break. The spec's In-Scope note ('non-bead message variants ... hold either way') is slightly loose on this point; the paired AC-global + falsification pin the correct constraint, so the plan is forced into the sound design.
R1: the pre-existing empty-bead RawMergeFence wart in instruct's own output is out of R1's scope (hygiene is CLI-verbs-only) and correctly left untouched — internally consistent.

R3: when --gate names a gate absent from panel.gates, PanelGateExpectedReviewers falls back through the R3 inheritance chain to the global count while still stamping the gate field — correct writer-side completion, not a defect.

R3: the changed expected_reviewers/approve_threshold at write time DO shift the decision, but that is via the recorded numbers (the sole decision inputs), not via the inert Gate field — the decision-inertness claim is precise and holds.
R2: `$` inside a JS regex char class is a literal, no escaping needed; $( stays matched — monotone confirmed.
